title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants two changes: (1) Add a `TestRenderPastePipeline_doesNotMutateInput` test to verify that mutating the returned map doesn't affect the input spec's middleware config, matching the existing immutability-verification test pattern already applied to `RenderPluginConfig`. (2) Add a doc comment (or guard) to `RenderINI` noting that empty section name keys produce invalid INI output (`[]`), since the function doesn't validate against this even though current callers happen to prevent it.

code_comments.body:
**`RenderPastePipeline` missing `_doesNotMutateInputs` test**

The project pattern (documented in the new `.planwerk/patterns/immutability-verification-test-for-map-returning-pure-functions.md` file) requires a `_doesNotMutateInput(s)` test for every pure function that returns a new map. The pattern is correctly applied to `RenderPluginConfig` (above, `TestRenderPluginConfig_doesNotMutateInput`), but `RenderPastePipeline` — which also returns `map[string]map[string]string` and deep-copies filter config values from `mw.Config` — has no equivalent.

The implementation does correctly create independent copies of each filter's `Config` entries (the `filterConfig` loop in `plugins.go`), so it is behaviourally safe. The gap is that there is no test asserting this, which means a future refactor that accidentally shares the `mw.Config` reference would not be caught.

A test in the style of the existing pattern would be:
```go
func TestRenderPastePipeline_doesNotMutateInput(t *testing.T) {
    g := NewGomegaWithT(t)

    spec := PipelineSpec{
        PipelineName: "api_v3",
        AppName:      "service_v3",
        BaseFilters:  []string{"cors"},
        Middleware: []types.MiddlewareSpec{
            {
                Name:          "audit",
                FilterFactory: "audit:factory",
                Position:      types.PipelinePositionBefore,
                Config:        map[string]string{"key": "value"},
            },
        },
    }

    result, err := RenderPastePipeline(spec)
    g.Expect(err).NotTo(HaveOccurred())

    // Mutating result should not affect spec.Middleware[0].Config.
    result["filter:audit"]["key"] = "mutated"
    result["filter:audit"]["new_key"] = "new_value"

    g.Expect(spec.Middleware[0].Config["key"]).To(Equal("value"))
    g.Expect(spec.Middleware[0].Config).NotTo(HaveKey("new_key"))
}
```

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added `TestRenderPastePipeline_doesNotMutateInputs` in `internal/common/plugins/plugins_test.go` following the suggested test structure: it constructs a `PipelineSpec` with middleware config, calls `RenderPastePipeline`, mutates the result map, and asserts the original spec's config is unchanged.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added a three-line documentation comment to `RenderINI` in `internal/common/config/config.go` stating that empty section names produce invalid `[]` headers and that callers are responsible for ensuring non-empty section names, matching the reviewer's suggested wording.

resolution_summary: Added a `TestRenderPastePipeline_doesNotMutateInputs` test to verify that mutating the returned map does not affect the input `PipelineSpec`. Added a documentation comment to `RenderINI` noting that empty section names produce invalid INI and that callers are responsible for ensuring non-empty names.
